item.title: Lightning doesn't take the shortest path
item.description: A simple physics experiment explores why lightning follows the path of least resistance, not the shortest route.

media:content.media:title: Lightning doesn't take the shortest path
media:content.media:description: A simple physics experiment explores why lightning follows the path of least resistance, not the shortest route.

item.title: Would you eat bugs?
item.description: In this video I eat a scorpion and some super bugs and crickets. Then I talk about why people should eat more bugs.

media:content.media:title: Would you eat bugs?
media:content.media:description: In this video I eat a scorpion and some super bugs and crickets. Then I talk about why people should eat more bugs.

item.title: Acme portable black hole
item.description: I show you my very own portable black hole made with IR flock sheet

item.title: What else can ferrofluid do?
item.description: I show you how to make an easy ferrocell

item.title: How do photochromic lenses work?
item.description: I show you how photochromic glass works

item.title: I split water molecules with my bare hands
item.description: In this video I show you how to split water molecules

item.title: The dark truth about chips
item.description: A science experiment reveals how chips change in a vacuum

item.title: How strong are spiders?
item.description: A science experiment explores spider strength and why these tiny creatures can be surprisingly powerful.

media:content.media:title: How strong are spiders?
media:content.media:description: A science experiment explores spider strength and why these tiny creatures can be surprisingly powerful.
